title: Atriplex fera

This family of plants is commonly known as the amaranth or pigweed family. The genus Atriplex is a group of saltbushes, which are characterized by their ability to thrive in saline environments. Atriplex fera is a type of shrub that is native to Australia and is often found in coastal areas. It is a halophyte, a plant adapted to grow in environments with high salt concentrations.
